title: Photometric Stereo (shape-from-shading with multiple light directions)
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is widefield-fluorescence template (fan-out artifact). Physics below is grounded in Woodham 1980 photometric stereo and canonical follow-up (robust PS, deep PS).

E.description: K images are captured under known directional light L_k, same camera pose. Under Lambertian reflectance, I_k(u,v) = rho(u,v) * max(0, n(u,v) . L_k); stacking gives I_stack = N * L^T where N = rho * n is the albedo-modulated normal. Least-squares gives N_hat; Poisson integration converts normals to a height field.
E.forward_model: I_k(u,v) = rho(u,v) * max(0, n(u,v) . L_k) * (1 - shadow_mask_k(u,v)) + I_ambient + n_k(u,v), k = 1..K
E.world_state_x: Surface normal map n(u,v) in S^2 on H x W and albedo rho(u,v); integrated height z(u,v) optional
E.observation_y: K images y_k(u,v) in R^{H x W x K} under distinct known light directions
physical_parameters_theta: Surface BRDF (Lambertian default; specular + SSS cause outliers)
Integration scheme for normals (Poisson, SPS, RANSAC-based Horn)

W.regime: With K >= 3 non-coplanar light directions and Lambertian BRDF, the linear system has rank 3 and is well-conditioned. Degrades for specular highlights (outlier fraction), cast and attached shadows (reduced effective K), non-Lambertian materials; robust methods (RANSAC, rank-3 SVD, learned PS) handle outliers up to ~30% specular fraction.

C.solver_class: Woodham least-squares, robust PCA rank-3, SVD / SFS, learned (PS-FCN, CNN-PS, DeepPS)
C.error_bound: sigma_normal ~ sigma_pixel / (rho * sqrt(K_valid)) where K_valid = K - shadow_count - specular_outliers; integration error ~ sqrt(area) * local normal error
C.complexity: O(H * W * K) for linear; O(N_iter * H * W * K) for robust PS
